intro: Monday's wrap covers the most consequential AI week of 2026 so far. Google committed up to $40 billion to Anthropic ($10B now, $30B contingent) at a $350B valuation — the largest single AI investment in history. DeepSeek V4 (1.6T parameters, Apache 2.0, ~1/7th the cost of GPT-5.5) dropped hours after OpenAI released GPT-5.5, turning Thursday into a two-front model war. Google Cloud Next 2026 unveiled the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, A2A cross-agent protocol, and new TPU 8i/8t chips — and PM Modi inaugurated India's AI Impact Summit with 500+ global leaders in New Delhi.

top_stories.headline: Google to Invest Up to $40 Billion in Anthropic — Largest Single AI Investment in History
top_stories.summary: Google announced it will invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic: $10 billion in cash now at a $350 billion valuation, plus $30 billion contingent on performance milestones, plus five gigawatts of Google Cloud compute over five years. Anthropic's annualised revenue has surpassed $30 billion — up from $9 billion at end of 2025 — making it the fastest-growing AI company on record. Amazon has separately committed up to $25 billion, meaning Anthropic now has up to $65 billion in committed external capital.

top_stories.headline: DeepSeek V4 Ships Hours After GPT-5.5 — 1.6T Apache 2.0 at One-Seventh the Cost
top_stories.summary: DeepSeek released V4-Pro and V4-Flash on April 24, hours after OpenAI released GPT-5.5. V4-Pro is a 1.6 trillion-parameter MoE (49B active) with a 1M-token context window under Apache 2.0, priced at roughly one-seventh of GPT-5.5 ($5/M input) and runs on Huawei Ascend chips. Performance trails GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7 on most benchmarks by a 3–6 month development gap. Tencent and Alibaba are in talks to take stakes in DeepSeek as the model becomes a national strategic asset.

top_stories.headline: Google Cloud Next 2026: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, A2A Protocol, and TPU 8i/8t
top_stories.summary: At Cloud Next 2026 in Las Vegas (April 22), Google rebranded Vertex AI as the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, shipped the production-grade Agent2Agent (A2A) cross-platform communication protocol, launched Project Mariner as a managed web-browsing agent, and announced TPU 8i (inference) and TPU 8t (training) delivering 2× better performance-per-watt over Ironwood. OpenAI released ChatGPT Workspace Agents the same day. Salesforce expanded its Google Cloud partnership to connect Agentforce with Gemini.

top_stories.headline: PM Modi Inaugurates India AI Impact Summit 2026 — New Delhi Declaration and $30B in Announcements
top_stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi opened the India AI Impact Summit 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi, with 500+ global leaders including 100 CEOs, 20+ heads of state, and leaders from every major AI company. The New Delhi Declaration on AI was signed, and IndiaAI Mission shortlisted 12 organisations — including Sarvam AI, IIT Bombay's BharatGen, and Tech Mahindra — to develop indigenous foundational models with government compute + funding support.

top_stories.headline: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5 and ChatGPT Workspace Agents — Enterprise AI War Goes Head-to-Head With Google
top_stories.summary: OpenAI shipped GPT-5.5 (priced at $5/M input, $30/M output for standard; $30/$180 for Pro) and ChatGPT Workspace Agents on April 22 — the same day as Google Cloud Next — triggering a simultaneous enterprise AI product war visible in real time. GPT-5.5 still leads most benchmarks over DeepSeek V4-Pro and Gemini 3.1, but the pricing gap is narrowing fast as open-weight competition intensifies.

quick_hits.text: Anthropic's annualised revenue crossed $30 billion this month — up from $9B at end of 2025 — driven by Claude Opus 4.7 adoption across Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex, and Azure, making it the fastest ARR ramp of any AI company.

Google launched Deep Research and Deep Research Max — AI agents that search the public web and private enterprise data simultaneously, directly competing with Perplexity's enterprise product and OpenAI's Deep Research feature.

Google's TPU 8i and 8t chips (announced at Cloud Next) deliver 2× better performance-per-watt than Ironwood and are optimised specifically for agentic AI workloads requiring long context windows and sequential reasoning.
VC deployed 1,314 deals in April 2026 — 764 (58%) involved AI or ML companies — continuing the AI funding supercycle that saw $242B deployed into AI in Q1 alone.

Tencent and Alibaba are in advanced talks to invest in DeepSeek's first external funding round, with Tencent proposing up to a 20% stake — a move that would make DeepSeek a jointly-backed Chinese national AI champion.

india_roundup.text: PM Modi's India AI Impact Summit 2026 produced the New Delhi Declaration on AI — a multilateral commitment signed by 20+ heads of state covering AI governance, sovereign infrastructure, and equitable access — positioning India as the Global South's AI policy leader.

IndiaAI Mission shortlisted 12 organisations to build indigenous foundational models: Sarvam AI, Soket AI, Gnani AI, IIT Bombay's BharatGen, Fractal Analytics, Tech Mahindra Maker's Lab, and six others — each receiving government compute + funding under the IndiaAI Innovation Challenge 2026.

NVIDIA deepened its India AI Mission partnership at the Summit — providing GPU infrastructure and developer tooling to all 12 IndiaAI Mission model-building organisations, expanding its India presence beyond data centres into the sovereign model layer.

India's IndiaAI Mission has now established 27 India Data and AI Labs and identified 543 additional labs for future development, supporting 8,000+ undergraduates, 5,000 postgraduates, and 500 PhD students in AI research.

India's AI and DPDP regulatory framework is set to drive a new phase of Big Tech compliance in 2026 — with mandatory AI content labelling, reduced takedown windows, and upcoming enforcement of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act shaping how OpenAI, Google, and Meta operate in the country.

closing_insight: In one week, Google committed $40B to Anthropic, DeepSeek shipped an open-weight frontier model at one-seventh the cost of GPT-5.5, and India signed a multilateral AI declaration with 20 heads of state. The polite infrastructure-sharing era of AI is completely over — every bet is now explicit, every alliance is now public, and the cost floor for frontier intelligence is dropping faster than anyone's revenue model assumed.
